what_it_does: A draft consolidation proposal (explicitly never executed) that analyzes three build-loop methodology docs (company-build, rhm-build, wire-build), establishes they are byte-identical mirrors of live runnable skills, catalogs their shared protocol skeleton and the drift between copies, and proposes ADDING a new one-source docs/methodology/build-loop-protocol.md page rather than merging or retiring any source. It includes the full proposed protocol text plus per-build deltas.
observations: States STATUS: DRAFT/PROPOSED ONLY at top; nothing executed, all sources untouched; owning session decides.
Documents an md5sum-verified 'mirror invariant': each repo doc is byte-identical to a live ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md and must change in lockstep (both-or-neither).
Estimates ~60-70% of each doc is a shared protocol skeleton: header discipline, files block, lanes, the 10-step fire, stopping, laws, invocation.

Catalogs concrete drift between the three copies (bridge restart hardening reached only 2 of 3, worker dispatch mode auto vs bypassPermissions, console-clean gate, refresh_self_description, scenario- vs unit-verify, channel-back presence).
Lists what each source uniquely holds (company lanes E/N/K/U/F; rhm S1-S7 + E3 channel-back; wire W6 unattended dispatch + fullest commit-law).
Notes branch rules in all three are superseded by the 2026-06-04 feedback-no-branches-company law (commit to main, no branches).
Includes a full proposed protocol page, a per-build delta section, a decisions table (D-a..D-e), and a disposition table.
Final verdict: mixed — extraction yes (add one page), replacement no (keep all three full); retire nothing.
standouts: The mirror-invariant argument: consolidating repo docs without identically restructuring live skills would manufacture the exact drift the lane exists to cure.
Frames the three docs as historical records of an EVOLVING protocol (company -> rhm -> wire), so normalizing onto one core would retroactively falsify what each loop actually ran.
The [VARIANT] annotation convention preserves divergent facts inline instead of erasing them on merge.
conventions: Follows the vault working-doc convention: explicit STATUS banner, RATIONALE, VERDICT, disposition table.
Self-describes as the deliverable of one consolidation lane with a named origin (DRIFT-RADAR-REPORT.md candidate #1, cos .86).
Uses ☐/✅/◑ marker glyphs and the repo's commit-law / no-Co-Authored-By trailer conventions in the embedded protocol text.
notes: A never-executed consolidation verdict that decides to ADD a canonical build-loop-protocol page while keeping all three mirror-of-skill docs intact, because they are simultaneously live-skill mirrors, self-contained cron prompts, and historical records.
questions: Has the owning session acted on any of the D-a..D-e decisions since this draft, or is it still purely proposed?
Were the md5sum matches re-verified after 2026-06-10, or could the mirror invariant already be stale?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it presents itself as a draft consolidation deliverable for one lane and delivers exactly that: rationale, verdict, proposed text, deltas, dispositions, all marked unexecuted.
apparent_themes: One-source / no-repeat law applied at the methodology layer
Preserving historical/as-ran fidelity (don't erase what actually ran)
Mirror invariants between repo docs and live skills
Fork-and-drift as the cost of copy-and-mutate reproduction
intention_signals: Trying to cure methodology-layer duplication without breaking the live-skill mirror or falsifying history — extract the shared build-loop protocol once as a future instantiation source and single landing place for improvements, while leaving every historical recipe intact.
novelty: The explicit reasoning that duplication here is partly BY DESIGN (declared lineage + durable-copy contract) and that 'three copies' are actually evolutionary snapshots, which inverts the usual dedup instinct.
contribution: Contributes a fully reasoned, evidence-grounded consolidation verdict for the build-loop methodology family plus a drafted canonical build-loop-protocol page with per-build deltas — a consolidated record of the cron/lanes/verify-by-use protocol and the precise unique facts each instantiation holds.
summary_for_embedding: A never-executed consolidation draft analyzing three byte-identical build-loop methodology docs and proposing to ADD a single canonical build-loop-protocol page (with per-build deltas) while keeping all sources intact.
intention_for_embedding: To resolve methodology-layer duplication additively by extracting the shared cron/lanes/verify-by-use build-loop protocol once, without breaking the repo-doc-to-live-skill mirror or erasing each loop's as-ran history.
